title: Claims for severance payments
content:
Notwithstanding anything in this Part, an employee shall not be 
entitled to a severance payment unless, before the end of the period 
of 3 months beginning with the relevant date, or within such 
extended period as the Commissioner may agree—  (Amended 19 
of 1984 s. 3)
	
(a)	
the payment has been agreed and paid;
	
(b)	
the employee has made a claim for payment by notice in 
writing given to the employer; or
	
(c)	
a question as to the right of the employee to the 
payment, or as to the amount of the payment, has been 
made the subject of a claim filed with—
	
(i)	
the Registrar of the Minor Employment Claims 
Adjudication Board in accordance with Part 4 of 
the Minor Employment Claims Adjudication Board 
Ordinance (Cap. 453); or
	
(ii)	
the Registrar of the Labour Tribunal in accordance 
with Part 4 of the Labour Tribunal Ordinance 
(Cap. 25). (Amended 61 of 1994 s. 50)
Last updated date
1.5.2025
Employment Ordinance
Part VA	
5A-28
Section 31M	
Cap. 57
Verified Copy
(Amended E.R. 1 of 2013)
